---
title: "Codebook"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# \*Full Variable Explanations (20 variables)\*\*

This codebook provides clear definitions for all 20 variables in the phone-usage dataset, including what each variable measures, its data type, and the possible levels for categorical responses. It also documents how student majors were grouped into **Business or Economics**, **Env–Bio–Health**, and **Other**, ensuring consistency in analysis and interpretation. Together, these descriptions create a coherent reference that supports accurate modeling, communication, and reproducibility.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **1. `timestamp`**

**Type:** Datetime (`POSIXct`) **Description:** The date and time when the student submitted the survey. **Levels:** N/A

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **2. `are_you_in_dr_williamss_fall_2025_stat_324_course`**

**Type:** Categorical (character) **Description:** Indicates whether the student is enrolled in STAT 324 for Fall 2025. **Levels:**

-   `"Yes"`
-   `"No"`

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **3. `age_years`**

**Type:** Numeric (`double`) **Description:** Student age in years. **Levels:** N/A

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **4. `what_is_your_major`**

**Type:** Categorical (character) **Description:** Student’s academic major, **recoded into three categories**. **Final Levels:**

### **A. Business or Economics**

-   Business Administration
-   Economics

### **B. Env–Bio–Health**

-   Environmental Management & Protection
-   Marine Science
-   Biological Sciences
-   Public Health
-   Child Development (optional but logically placed here)

### **C. Other**

-   City & Regional Planning
-   Computer Science
-   Mathematics
-   Mechanical Engineering
-   MSCI (Industrial Tech / Manufacturing Science / Industrial Engineering–style)
-   Statistics
-   Any remaining major not in A or B

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **5. `class_standing`**

**Type:** Categorical **Description:** Student’s current class level. **Typical Levels:**

-   `"Freshman"`
-   `"Sophomore"`
-   `"Junior"`
-   `"Senior"`
-   `"Graduate"`

(Actual levels depend on your dataset but follow this pattern.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **6. `phone_operating_system`**

**Type:** Categorical **Description:** The OS of the student’s phone. **Levels:**

-   `"iOS (iPhone)"`
-   `"Android"`
-   (possible: `"Other"` if someone entered it manually)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **7. `phone_brand`**

**Type:** Categorical **Description:** The brand of the student’s phone. **Examples of levels:**

-   `"Apple"`
-   `"Samsung"`
-   `"Google"`
-   `"Motorola"`
-   `"Other"` (Exact levels depend on your dataset.)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **8. `can_you_access_a_weekly_phone_usage_report_on_your_device`**

**Type:** Categorical **Description:** Whether the student can access a weekly screen-time report on their phone. **Levels:**

-   `"Yes"`
-   `"No"`

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **9. `avg_screen_time_min`**

**Type:** Numeric **Description:** Average daily screen time over the last 7 days (in minutes). **Levels:** N/A

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **10. `total_screen_time_7d_min`**

**Type:** Numeric **Description:** Total screen time in minutes over the last 7 days. **Levels:** N/A

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **11. `avg_unlocks_day`**

**Type:** Numeric **Description:** Average number of daily unlocks/pickups over the last 7 days. **Levels:** N/A

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **12. `avg_notifications_day`**

**Type:** Numeric **Description:** Average number of notifications received per day over the last 7 days. **Levels:** N/A

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **13. `do_you_go_on_instagram_this_includes_finstas`**

**Type:** Categorical **Description:** Whether the student uses Instagram (including fake or secondary accounts). **Levels:**

-   `"Yes"`
-   `"No"`

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **14. `ig_minutes_7d`**

**Type:** Numeric **Description:** Total Instagram minutes used over the last 7 days. **Levels:** N/A

*(0 means they do not use Instagram.)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **15. `do_you_go_on_tik_tok_fake_accounts_count_here_as_well`**

**Type:** Categorical **Description:** Whether the student uses TikTok (including fake or secondary accounts). **Levels:**

-   `"Yes"`
-   `"No"`

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **16. `tiktok_minutes_7d`**

**Type:** Numeric **Description:** Total TikTok minutes used over the last 7 days. **Levels:** N/A

*(0 means they do not use TikTok.)*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **17. `other_social_count`**

**Type:** Numeric **Description:** Number of **other** social media platforms (besides IG & TikTok) used at least once per week. Examples include: Facebook, Snapchat, LinkedIn, Reddit, X/Twitter, BeReal…

Levels: numeric counts (0, 1, 2, …)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **18. `i_feel_like_i_use_my_phone_more_than_i_should`**

**Type:** Categorical **Description:** Likert-scale response about perceived overuse. **Typical Levels:**

-   `"Strongly Disagree"`
-   `"Disagree"`
-   `"Neutral"`
-   `"Agree"`
-   `"Strongly Agree"`

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **19. `social_media_increases_my_stress`**

**Type:** Categorical **Description:** Likert-scale response about stress caused by social media. **Typical Levels:**

-   `"Strongly Disagree"`
-   `"Disagree"`
-   `"Neutral"`
-   `"Agree"`
-   `"Strongly Agree"`

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **20. `social_media_helps_me_feel_more_connected_to_other_people`**

**Type:** Categorical **Description:** Likert-scale response about social connection. **Typical Levels:**

-   `"Strongly Disagree"`
-   `"Disagree"`
-   `"Neutral"`
-   `"Agree"`
-   `"Strongly Agree"`
